title: Bank Transfer Transaction Example
description: Demonstrates ACID properties in a fund transfer operation
code:
BEGIN TRANSACTION
 READ(balance_A FROM Account_A)
 IF balance_A < 1000 THEN
 ROLLBACK
 RETURN "Insufficient funds"
 END IF
 
 balance_A = balance_A - 1000
 WRITE(balance_A TO Account_A)
 
 READ(balance_B FROM Account_B)
 balance_B = balance_B + 1000
 WRITE(balance_B TO Account_B)
 
 COMMIT
 RETURN "Transfer successful"
END TRANSACTION
explanation: This shows atomicity: both debit and credit happen together or not at all. Consistency: balance constraints maintained. Isolation: other transfers see either original or final state. Durability: once committed, changes persist.

title: Precedence Graph Construction Algorithm
description: Algorithm to build precedence graph and check for serializability
code:
// Input: Schedule S with n transactions
// Output: Serializable or Not Serializable

FUNCTION buildPrecedenceGraph(Schedule S):
 Graph G = empty directed graph
 Transactions = getAllTransactions(S)
 
 FOR each transaction Ti in Transactions:
 addNode(G, Ti)
 
 FOR each pair of conflicting operations (Oi, Oj):
 // Oi in Ti, Oj in Tj, i ≠ j
 IF Oi executes before Oj AND they conflict THEN
 addEdge(G, Ti, Tj)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN G

FUNCTION checkSerializability(Graph G):
 IF hasCycle(G) THEN
 RETURN "Not Serializable"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Serializable"
 END IF
explanation: The algorithm builds a directed graph where edges represent conflicts. A cycle indicates non-serializability because it means circular dependencies exist between transactions that cannot be resolved serially.

title: Two-Phase Locking Protocol
description: Demonstrates the growing and shrinking phases in 2PL
code:
// Two-Phase Locking Protocol

FUNCTION executeTransaction(T):
 // Growing Phase
 WHILE T has operations DO:
 operation = getNextOperation(T)
 IF operation is READ THEN
 lock = SHARED_LOCK(data_item)
 ELSE // WRITE
 lock = EXCLUSIVE_LOCK(data_item)
 END IF
 IF lock GRANTED THEN
 execute(operation)
 ELSE
 WAIT FOR lock
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 // Shrinking Phase - Begins when first lock is released
 releaseAllLocks()
 
 COMMIT

// Note: Locks cannot be acquired after shrinking phase starts
// This ensures conflict serializability
explanation: 2PL ensures serializability by dividing execution into growing phase (locks acquired) and shrinking phase (locks released). Once first lock released, no new locks can be acquired.
